surface_verification.runner: Playwright against an isolated single-process stack (temp-USERPROFILE Hono :3850 serving the built SPA): e2e/flows/95-compliance-grade.spec.ts + regression 03-projects / 35-no-chat-panel / 49-project-filter

behaviors.behavior: parseDashboard extracts grade/score/verdict/generatedAt from the real dashboard fixture
parseDashboard slices Control-Verdict + CI-Security sections, excludes the trailing Compliance Artifacts table, is fence-aware and leading-whitespace tolerant
behaviors.evidence: compliance-reader.test.ts (section slices, fenced-code boundary, whitespace tolerance) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: GET /compliance returns the ok payload AND passes the resolved project.path into the reader
GET /compliance 404 for unknown project, 400 for a resolvable path-less project, status passthrough for missing/invalid
ComplianceGradeBadge maps grade A->emerald / B->amber / <=C->red and exposes verdict + generated date in the tooltip
Badge renders nothing on missing/invalid/loading and stops click propagation to a clickable parent row
Clicking the badge opens the Radix dialog (role=dialog) rendering the dimension + CI tables via DocumentMarkdown; empty CI slice degrades gracefully
behaviors.evidence: ComplianceGradeBadge.test.tsx (dialog open) + ComplianceDetailModal.test.tsx (GFM table render) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: End-to-end: seeded project's dashboard -> route -> reader -> Projects Grade column badge renders, opens modal with dimension table, Compliance Artifacts excluded (full chain through real DOM)
Exact pixel fidelity of the rendered markdown tables vs the dashboard
behaviors.reason: GFM-table visual fidelity is a human-eye judgment; presence of the table content is asserted instead (modal + E2E)
